findings.finding: TC1 (no new persistent state) is load-bearing for the R4/R5 consolidation-bypass bug. Cleanly separating pre-consolidation research-quality signal from post-consolidation ack flow requires tracking both counts during the decompose run. TC1 pushes toward a single evaluation point which is precisely what creates the laundering path.
findings.evidence_quote: TC1 — Rule-edit scope only: No new files, tooling, or persistent state.

findings.finding: R2 surface-pattern list is closed-form and omits generic-authority phrasings (recommended, standard, widely adopted, accepted, conventional). An agent aware of the gate evades by substituting generic authority for named vendor. The list needs either expansion or a shift to a non-lexical signal.
findings.evidence_quote: external-endorsement surface patterns (e.g., vendor X recommends, Anthropic says, CrewAI docs, industry best practice, canonical pattern in $framework)

findings.finding: R2(a) written-by-agent-at-decompose-time (not copied verbatim from research.md) has no enforcement mechanism. Same-model agent trivially paraphrases. Requirement is observable only by human reviewer post-hoc, which is the escape hatch the gate is meant to reduce.
findings.evidence_quote: produce a [file:line] citation that grounds the Value claim in this codebase, written by the agent at decompose time (not copied verbatim from research.md)

findings.finding: §6 Decomposition Record has no dropped-items section; flagged-and-dropped items leave no trace in the durable accounting artifact. Re-running /discovery on same topic cannot detect the same unverified premise was already proposed and rejected, enabling silent repeat-and-drop cycles with no captured learning.
findings.reviewer_angle: Silent scope-shift from Drop this item
findings.evidence_quote: Decomposition outputs go to research/{topic}/decomposed.md as an accounting record (per §6 of decompose.md)
